basic_details.job_title: OSSC CTSRE Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 646 Junior Engineer, Tracer and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Odisha Staff Selection Commission (OSSC), Unit-II, Bhubaneswar
basic_details.application_mode: Online only via www.ossc.gov.in. No Physical copy/Hard copy of Online Application Form to be submitted.
basic_details.short_information: OSSC is recruiting 646 posts under CTSRE-2025 for Group-B technical services with Diploma/Degree qualification, last date 29 June 2026.

important_dates.exam_date: To be notified after Preliminary Examination result

application_fee.general_fee: Rs. 500/- (Refundable on appearing in Preliminary Examination)
application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay Examination fees through online mode only via the Odisha Treasury portal linked with the online application form. The examination fee paid shall be refunded to candidates who actually appear in Stage-I (Preliminary Examination). Applications without fee payment (except SC/ST/PwD candidates) will be treated as incomplete and liable for rejection. All candidates must mention their bank account details in the online application form to receive the refund.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be a minimum of 21 years and maximum of 42 years as on 01-01-2025, i.e., candidates must not have been born earlier than 2nd January 1983 and not later than 1st January 2004 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of Odisha guidelines.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date of submission of Online Application Form. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must be able to read, write and speak Odia fluently and must have passed Middle School Examination with Odia as a language subject, or HSC with Odia as medium, or passed Odia as language subject in Class-VII or above from a school recognised by Govt. of Odisha/Central Govt., or passed a test in Odia at Middle English School standard conducted by School and Mass Education Department. | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

vacancy_details.Department/Office: Director, Panchayati Raj, Panchayati Raj & Drinking Water Department

salary_details.pay_scale: Level-9, Cell-1 / Level-8, Cell-1 / Level-5, Cell-1 of ORSP, 2017 (as applicable per post)

selection_process: Sl. Subjects Total Marks Duration Mode

1 One Paper: Arithmetic (10th standard), Data Interpretation (Chart, Graph, Table, Data Sufficiency – 10th standard), Logical Reasoning and Analytical Ability, General Mental Ability, Current Events of National and International Importance, Computer/Internet Awareness 150 Marks 150 minutes MCQ (OMR / CBRE mode)
Sl. Paper Total Marks Duration Remarks

1 Technical Paper (syllabus as per Annexure-D of notification; Technical Paper for Junior Draughtsman (Civil) and Tracer will be the same) 200 Marks 2 hours (120 minutes) Different papers for different posts. Candidates numbering 1.5 times the advertised vacancies (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for Certificate Verification. Negative marking: 1/4 of total mark (4 options) or 1/3 of total mark (3 options)
Selection shall be based on three stages: (i) Preliminary Examination

and (iii) Certificate Verification. Allotment to a post/service shall be made on the basis of merit-cum-preference. Candidates must appear in all stages; absence at any stage will lead to rejection of candidature. Question papers shall be in both Odia and English versions; candidates must choose their medium while filling the online application form (option once given cannot be changed).

Negative marking: 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. A minimum of 5 times the number of vacancies (post-wise and category-wise) shall be shortlisted for Main Written Examination. If vacancies in any post/category are less than 10
then candidates numbering 10 times the vacancies shall be shortlisted.
Candidates shortlisted after Main Written Examination shall attend Certificate Verification. Candidates must produce original academic certificates
and other documents as required. Absence at Certificate Verification will result in cancellation of candidature. No request for change of date will be entertained.

physical_eligibility.details: The competition level for OSSC CTSRE 2025 is expected to be high given the 646 Group-B/C technical vacancies open to diploma holders in civil engineering, architecture, and media/AV disciplines across Odisha. The Preliminary Examination, being a general aptitude paper at 10th standard level, will attract a wide pool of aspirants including those already preparing for other state-level exams. The largest chunk of vacancies — 606 seats — is under Junior Engineer (Civil) across three departments, making it the most sought-after post. | The effective competition narrows significantly at Stage-II as the Technical Paper is post-specific and tests core subject knowledge, naturally filtering out those with weak technical preparation. Candidates with strong AutoCAD, computer literacy, or AV production skills will have a clear edge in the specialty posts. Since allotment is based on merit-cum-preference, accuracy in both stages and a well-thought-out preference list are critical to securing the desired post and department.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only through the official website www.ossc.gov.in; no other mode of application will be accepted. No physical copy/hard copy of the online application form needs to be submitted. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date. Important instructions on filling the Online Application Form and "How to Apply" are enclosed as Annexure-A to the official notification.
Documents Required at the time of Examination / Certificate Verification:
Admission Letter (downloaded from www.ossc.gov.in) along with valid photo identity proof in original and photocopy
Original academic certificates and marksheets (relevant Diploma/Degree/Certificate onwards)
Caste Certificate in the prescribed Govt. of Odisha format (for SEBC/SC/ST candidates)
Disability Certificate / UDID Card issued by competent Medical Authority (for PwD candidates) — scanned copy of original to be uploaded at application stage
Sports ID Card issued by Director of Sports & Youth Service Department, Govt. of Odisha (for Sports Person candidates)
No Objection Certificate (NOC) from competent controlling authority (for Government servants / PSU employees)
Any other documents as intimated in the Admission Letter for Certificate Verification (details in Annexure-B of notification)
For queries: Email [email protected] (complaints on examination conduct to be sent within 05 days of examination completion).
important_instructions: The recruitment shall be guided by "Combined Technical Services Recruitment Examination Rules-2022" as amended from time to time. Candidates must possess a valid email ID and mobile number while applying and keep them active till completion of the recruitment process to receive important messages from the Commission. The appointment will be only against one of the posts carrying the corresponding pay as indicated in the vacancy table; candidates applying for more than one post/service must give option/preference for the post/service in case they qualify. The Commission will adopt a "Normalisation Formula" published vide Notice No.2444/OSSC dated 02.09.2021 for processing results if any examination is conducted through CBRE/OMR mode in multiple batches using different sets of question papers. No Admission Letter for recruitment at any stage shall be sent by post; candidates must download from www.ossc.gov.in regularly for updates. Candidates are advised to submit the Online Application Form well in advance without waiting for the closing date to avoid last-hour rush. All posts are State Cadre posts; the candidate on selection may be asked to serve anywhere in Odisha. OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates are not entitled to SEBC reservation; they must apply under UR category. PwD candidates must ensure they possess a permanent disability certificate issued online and upload the scanned copy of the original (not photocopy) of the Disability Certificate/UDID Card. There shall be no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores; no correspondence in this regard shall be entertained. Mobile phones, Bluetooth devices, spy cameras, smartwatches and any other electronic gadgets are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall. In case of tie in scores, merit will be decided by: (i) Marks in Preliminary Examination, (ii) Date of Birth with older candidate placed higher, and (iii) Alphabetical order of names. The Commission's decision in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance/rejection of applications, mode of selection, conduct of examination, allotment of examination centres and preparation of merit list shall be final and binding. Any complaint on the conduct of the examination must be sent to [email protected] within 05 (five) days of completion of examination. Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.

faq.question: Q1. What is OSSC CTSRE 2025 Recruitment?
faq.answer: OSSC CTSRE 2025 is a Combined Technical Services Recruitment Examination conducted by the Odisha Staff Selection Commission to fill 646 Group-B and Group-C technical posts across various departments.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: A total of 646 vacancies have been announced, mainly for Junior Engineer (Civil) along with posts like Architectural Assistant, Cameraman, and others.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: The last date for online registration and fee payment is 29 June 2026, while the final submission deadline is 2 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Diploma/Degree in relevant fields (like Civil Engineering, Architecture, or Media-related disciplines) and must be able to read, write, and speak Odia.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Applicants must be between 21 to 42 years as on 01 January 2025, with age relaxation applicable for reserved categories as per Odisha government rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: The selection process includes: Preliminary Examination (Objective) Main Written Examination (Technical) Certificate Verification

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Yes, ₹500 for General/SEBC candidates (refundable after appearing in prelims), while SC/ST/PwD candidates are exempted.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern of OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: The prelim exam consists of 150 MCQs (150 marks), and the mains includes a 200-mark technical paper. Negative marking applies in both stages.

faq.question: Q9. Can candidates from other states apply for OSSC CTSRE 2025?
faq.answer: Yes, but only Indian citizens are eligible. However, candidates must fulfill the Odia language requirement to qualify.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for OSSC CTSRE 2025 online?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official website ossc.gov.in by completing registration, filling out the application form, uploading documents, and paying the fee.

title: OSSC CTSRE Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 646 Junior Engineer, Tracer and More Posts
